refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (vehicle control, teleoperation, safety monitoring) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational scenarios.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials (e.g., standardized earthmoving scenarios) with instrumented telemetry logging productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h), operator workload, safety events, and task completion time. Compare against productivity_target and document compliance with worksite safety plan.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and material handling capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain target productivity.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The teleoperation system (if deployed) shall maintain command latency and telemetry fidelity within limits that preserve operator effectiveness.
FR-NGHE-001.4: Safety monitoring and alerting shall detect and communicate hazards without disrupting task execution below the productivity target.
FR-NGHE-001.5: System telemetry shall continuously measure and log productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle time) for real-time and post-operation analysis.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-capacity vehicle with optimized hydraulic actuation, bucket geometry, and load-sensing control to maximize material throughput per cycle; pair with on-site operator cabin providing direct line-of-sight and tactile feedback.

DP-B: Deploy a teleoperated vehicle with remote operator station connected via low-latency network link; vehicle equipped with multi-sensor suite (cameras, LIDAR, IMU) and autonomous task planning to compensate for teleoperation latency; safety services monitor both vehicle state and operator commands.

DP-C: Hybrid approach: vehicle capable of both on-site and remote operation modes; operator selects mode based on task and site conditions; teleoperation system provides fallback when on-site operation is unsafe or impractical; integrated safety services monitor both modes.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid on-site/remote operation with mode-selectable control architecture, multi-sensor perception (cameras, LIDAR, IMU), low-latency network link for teleoperation, autonomous task assistance, and integrated safety services monitoring vehicle state, operator commands, and worksite hazards.

potential_sources: Vehicle actuation (hydraulic/electric) couples to operator control interface responsiveness; must ensure control loop bandwidth supports target cycle time.
Teleoperation latency couples to operator situational awareness and task execution speed; latency > ~500ms degrades productivity.
Safety alerting couples to task execution; overly aggressive safety constraints may prevent reaching productivity_target.
Telemetry fidelity couples to productivity measurement accuracy; insufficient sensor coverage may mask true productivity.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C is a coupled design (multiple FRs depend on shared subsystems: vehicle, network, sensors, safety logic). Coupling is acceptable at this level because the hybrid architecture is a deliberate trade-off between flexibility and complexity. Decoupling would require separate vehicles or networks, which is economically infeasible. Recommend detailed coupling analysis at next decomposition level.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Network infrastructure at customer site supports low-latency (<200ms round-trip) teleoperation link; Risk: If latency exceeds 500ms, remote operator productivity degrades significantly.

Assumption: Vehicle hydraulic/electric actuation can be controlled with sufficient precision and responsiveness for both on-site and remote modes; Risk: Teleoperation mode may require additional damping or rate-limiting, reducing peak productivity.

Assumption: Multi-sensor suite (cameras, LIDAR, IMU) provides sufficient redundancy for safe operation in both modes; Risk: Sensor failures or environmental occlusion (dust, rain) may degrade situational awareness.

Assumption: Safety services can distinguish between legitimate task execution and hazardous conditions without false positives; Risk: Over-conservative safety logic may halt operations below productivity_target.
Assumption: Operator training and workload management are within scope of customer operations; Risk: Inadequate operator training may limit effective use of teleoperation mode.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly specify: (1) network latency budget and fallback strategy if latency exceeds threshold; (2) sensor redundancy and failure modes; (3) safety alert thresholds and override procedures; (4) operator mode-selection criteria and training requirements. These refinements will reduce coupling and clarify allocation to sub-systems.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module encompassing all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their interfaces. It is responsible for: (1) coordinating mode selection (on-site vs. remote operation); (2) managing telemetry aggregation and productivity metric calculation; (3) ensuring safety services are active in both modes; (4) providing operator interface (on-site cabin or remote station); (5) managing network link and fallback strategies.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task definitions from Site Manager or Tele-operator (e.g., 'excavate zone A', 'load truck B', 'dump at location C'). Tasking includes task type, location, material type, and priority.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits real-time and logged telemetry to Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality systems. Includes vehicle state (position, orientation, bucket load, hydraulic pressure), operator commands, productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle time), sensor data (cameras, LIDAR, IMU), and safety events.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Transmits safety alerts to Safety Manager and on-site/remote operator. Includes hazard type (collision risk, stability risk, operator fatigue, environmental hazard), severity level, recommended action, and timestamp.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel between on-site vehicle and remote operator station (if teleoperation mode is active). Carries operator commands (control inputs) and vehicle telemetry (sensor data, state feedback). Latency budget: <200ms round-trip for nominal operation; graceful degradation up to 500ms; fallback to on-site mode if >500ms.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for the entire productivity KPI requirement. It owns the mode-selection logic, telemetry aggregation, and operator interface coordination. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary. Internal subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are fully contained.

Vehicle module owns the physical actuation and task execution (dig, load, transport, dump), which directly contributes to productivity. However, productivity also depends on operator control (on-site or remote), safety constraints, and telemetry measurement. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for operator interface, teleoperation latency, and safety monitoring.

Teleoperation module owns remote operator interface and network link, which are critical for remote productivity. However, teleoperation is optional (on-site mode is also supported), and productivity depends on vehicle capability and safety constraints. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Teleoperation alone would exclude on-site operation and vehicle performance factors.

Safety Services owns hazard detection and alerting, which constrains productivity (e.g., safety alerts may halt operations). However, Safety Services does not directly execute tasks or measure productivity. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Safety Services would be a poor fit; Safety Services is a constraint on productivity, not a driver of it.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The productivity KPI (FR-NGHE-001) is a system-level requirement that integrates vehicle capability, operator interface (on-site or remote), safety constraints, and telemetry measurement. No single subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) can independently satisfy this requirement. The NGHE Solution module, as the top-level integration container, is the appropriate owner. It will decompose FR-NGHE-001 into sub-FRs allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services at the next level.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration module with significant internal complexity (mode selection, telemetry aggregation, operator interface coordination). If not further decomposed, it may become a 'black box' that obscures responsibility and testing strategy. Mitigation: Decompose NGHE Solution into sub-modules (e.g., Mode Controller, Telemetry Aggregator, Operator Interface Manager) at the next level.

Risk: Productivity measurement (telemetry) is a critical input to verifying FR-NGHE-001, but telemetry is also an external interface (output). If telemetry is not properly instrumented or calibrated, productivity metrics may be inaccurate, leading to false acceptance or rejection. Mitigation: Define telemetry measurement requirements (sensor types, calibration, logging frequency) as part of DP refinement.

Risk: Safety Services may impose constraints that prevent reaching productivity_target (e.g., overly conservative hazard thresholds). If Safety Services is not properly tuned, the system may be 'safe but unproductive'. Mitigation: Establish safety-productivity trade-off criteria and validate during representative operations trials.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine IM assignment to explicitly define: (1) NGHE Solution internal sub-modules (Mode Controller, Telemetry Aggregator, Operator Interface Manager, Safety Coordinator); (2) interfaces between sub-modules and external modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services); (3) telemetry measurement requirements (sensor types, calibration, logging frequency, accuracy); (4) safety-productivity trade-off criteria and validation strategy.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid on-site/remote operation with integrated safety services) directly addresses FR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI). The DP specifies how to achieve target productivity through: (1) vehicle capability (material throughput per cycle); (2) operator interface flexibility (on-site or remote); (3) teleoperation system with low-latency network link and sensor perception; (4) safety services that monitor and alert without disrupting task execution. The DP is feasible, testable, and aligned with stakeholder needs (Site Manager, Operator, Tele-operator, Safety Manager).

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Mode selection logic (on-site vs. remote) is a critical control function that must be owned by a single module to avoid race conditions or inconsistent state. NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner, but the logic must be clearly specified and tested.

Diffusion Risk 2: Telemetry aggregation (combining vehicle state, operator commands, sensor data, safety events) is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. NGHE Solution must define a clear telemetry schema and aggregation strategy to avoid data inconsistencies.

Diffusion Risk 3: Safety Services operates in both on-site and remote modes, but the hazard detection logic may differ between modes (e.g., remote mode may have additional latency-related hazards). NGHE Solution must ensure Safety Services is mode-aware.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate container for FR-NGHE-001 because it integrates all subsystems and owns the mode-selection, telemetry aggregation, and safety coordination logic. The module boundary is clear: external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined, and internal subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained. Containment score of 95 is justified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: FR-NGHE-001 is verifiable through representative operations trials with instrumented telemetry logging. Productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle time) can be measured directly from vehicle sensors and task logs. Safety compliance can be verified through safety event logs and worksite safety plan audit. Operator effectiveness can be assessed through workload metrics and task completion time. Verification is feasible and repeatable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution, which will decompose it into sub-FRs for Vehicle (task execution capability), Teleoperation (remote operator interface), and Safety Services (hazard monitoring). The allocation is clear and traceable. Each sub-FR will have a corresponding DP and IM assignment at the next decomposition level.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Refine DP-C to specify network latency budget, sensor redundancy, safety alert thresholds, and operator mode-selection criteria. This will reduce ambiguity and support detailed design.

Revision 2: Define telemetry measurement requirements (sensor types, calibration, logging frequency, accuracy) as part of DP refinement. This will ensure productivity metrics are accurate and verifiable.
Revision 3: Establish safety-productivity trade-off criteria and validation strategy. This will clarify how to balance safety constraints with productivity targets.

Revision 4: Decompose NGHE Solution into sub-modules (Mode Controller, Telemetry Aggregator, Operator Interface Manager, Safety Coordinator) at the next level. This will reduce complexity and clarify responsibility.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level, coupled system requirement that cannot be fully satisfied by a single subsystem. The refined FR and selected DP are sound, and the IM assignment to NGHE Solution is appropriate. However, the requirement is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-FRs for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify responsibility, reduce coupling, and support detailed design and verification.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and material handling capacity sufficient to achieve ≥ productivity_target when operated by a trained operator.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site cabin or remote station) shall provide sufficient situational awareness, control responsiveness, and ergonomic design to enable a trained operator to sustain target productivity without excessive workload or fatigue.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The teleoperation system shall maintain command latency and telemetry fidelity within limits that preserve operator effectiveness and allow remote operation to achieve ≥ 80% of on-site productivity.

FR-NGHE-001.4: Safety monitoring and alerting shall detect and communicate hazards (collision risk, stability risk, operator fatigue, environmental hazard) without disrupting task execution below the productivity target, except when hazard severity exceeds acceptable risk thresholds.

FR-NGHE-001.5: System telemetry shall continuously measure and log productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle time, material type, task type) with ≥ 95% accuracy for real-time and post-operation analysis.

assumptions: Assumption 1: Customer site has network infrastructure supporting low-latency (<200ms round-trip) teleoperation link. If not available, remote operation productivity will degrade.

Assumption 2: Vehicle hydraulic/electric actuation can be controlled with sufficient precision and responsiveness for both on-site and remote modes. Teleoperation mode may require additional damping or rate-limiting.
Assumption 3: Multi-sensor suite (cameras, LIDAR, IMU) provides sufficient redundancy for safe operation in both modes. Sensor failures or environmental occlusion may degrade situational awareness.

Assumption 4: Safety Services can distinguish between legitimate task execution and hazardous conditions without false positives. Over-conservative safety logic may halt operations below productivity_target.
Assumption 5: Operator training and workload management are within scope of customer operations. Inadequate operator training may limit effective use of teleoperation mode.
Assumption 6: Productivity_target is defined by customer and is achievable with current vehicle and operator technology. If target is unrealistic, system design cannot compensate.
open_risks: Risk 1 (High): Network latency exceeds 500ms, causing remote operator productivity to degrade below acceptable levels. Mitigation: Conduct network latency testing at customer site before deployment; establish fallback strategy (e.g., switch to on-site mode or autonomous task execution).

Risk 2 (High): Safety Services imposes constraints that prevent reaching productivity_target. Mitigation: Establish safety-productivity trade-off criteria during design; validate during representative operations trials.

Risk 3 (Medium): Telemetry measurement inaccuracy leads to false acceptance or rejection of productivity KPI. Mitigation: Define telemetry measurement requirements and calibration procedures; validate against independent measurement methods.

Risk 4 (Medium): Operator workload in remote mode exceeds acceptable levels, causing fatigue and reduced productivity. Mitigation: Conduct operator workload assessment during design; provide autonomous task assistance to reduce workload.

Risk 5 (Medium): Sensor failures or environmental occlusion (dust, rain) degrade situational awareness in remote mode. Mitigation: Implement sensor redundancy and failure detection; provide fallback to on-site mode if sensor suite is degraded.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Network latency measurements at customer site (round-trip latency, jitter, packet loss rate).
Evidence 2: Vehicle cycle time and material throughput measurements under on-site and remote operation modes.
Evidence 3: Operator workload assessment (NASA Task Load Index or similar) for on-site and remote operation modes.
Evidence 4: Safety event logs and hazard detection accuracy (true positive rate, false positive rate) during representative operations trials.
Evidence 5: Telemetry measurement accuracy validation (comparison with independent measurement methods).
Evidence 6: Productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle time) logged during representative operations trials, compared against productivity_target.
Evidence 7: Worksite safety plan compliance audit and safety manager sign-off.
